round1: Round 1 is in the books, and for most of you, so is any hope of bragging rights. While Marc, Jaclyn, and Ryan scraped together a respectable lead, the rest of you are already proving that picking Toronto, Edmonton, or Pittsburgh was a fast track to the bottom. Theodore and Jamie, enjoy the view from last place; at least you tried... probably.
round2: Round 2 was an absolute bloodbath of upsets, proving most of you know less about hockey than my grandma. Theodore, you're the only one who didn't embarrass yourself, nailing all four series and even the Habs sweep. Meanwhile, Stephanie and Ryan, you might want to consider picking lottery numbers instead, because hockey clearly isn't your sport.
round3: Round 3 is wrapped, and it seems some of you are better at watching than predicting. While Stephanie, Jamie, and Derrick managed to actually get a series right, Benedict, Ryan, and Theodore collectively proved that picking with your eyes closed is a guaranteed path to absolute failure.
round4: Tampa Bay took the Cup in five games. Stephanie nailed it, making everyone else who backed Montreal look like they've never watched hockey. Special mention to Ryan for getting the game count right for the wrong team, and to those predicting a Habs sweep – you're officially in the penalty box.
overall: Alright, the 2021 NHL playoffs are officially in the books, and while the Tampa Bay Lightning celebrated their second consecutive Stanley Cup, our pool crowned Stephanie its champion with a truly astounding 18 points, making this the lowest-scoring victory in recent memory. After two years of bottom-feeding, Stephanie managed to pull off a comeback, proving that even in a field of mediocrity, someone has to win. Down at the bottom, a disgraceful half-dozen—Theodore, Benedict, Derrick, Jake, Kiersten, and Robin—pulled off the remarkable feat of scoring zero points, with Robin achieving the dubious distinction of back-to-back last-place finishes. Former champions Marc and Nathan, fresh off a 2020 win, looked like they were picking names out of a hat blindfolded, landing in a dismal four-way tie for fourth with perennial non-winner Nickall. Honestly, this year’s pool was less about hockey acumen and more about who could stumble across the finish line with the fewest embarrassing missteps.
